decisions.summary: Single-branch hard cut. No backward-compatibility shims, no readRole() migration helper, no soft transition. On upgrade: in-flight agents are killed, state.json files lacking `role` are dropped, config.yaml `overrides` (legacy WorkTypeId map) is silently dropped, defaults seeded for missing workhorses/roles. Users restart any active issues against the new pipeline.

Workhorse Models = exactly 3 user-managed slots (`expensive`, `mid`, `cheap`) at the top of Settings. Every per-role and per-sub-role model picker accepts EITHER a literal model id OR a `workhorse:<slot>` reference. Default config uses workhorse refs everywhere — fresh install fully managed through the 3 dials. Resolution dereferences at spawn time (live cascade, not frozen at picker-click). Unresolved or chained references fail loudly at config-load time with a precise field-pointer error.

Plan and Review default to `workhorse:expensive`. Rationale: planning errors cascade through every downstream bead (Jidoka can catch wrong implementation but not wrong foundation); review is the decision-maker that gates merge. Both deserve the strongest model. Work/Test/Ship default to `workhorse:mid`. Inspect (every-bead self-check) defaults to `workhorse:cheap`; inspect-deep (flagged beads) to `workhorse:mid`.

Drop work phases entirely. The work agent today transitions through exploration → implementation → testing → documentation → review-response with potentially different models per phase. After this PR, work is one undifferentiated mode with one model (workhorse:mid by default) plus two inspect sub-roles. All `state.phase` / `state.workType` reads die. Phase-handoff plumbing is removed.

Reactive Cloister hooks into existing internal event emitters — NOT a new tracker poller. Issue-lifecycle service (src/dashboard/server/services/issue-lifecycle.ts) already emits `issue.*` domain events. Cloister subscribes to those plus existing agent-completion events (agent.completed, work.completed, review.approved, test.passed) and uses stateToRole(IssueState) to spawn the next role. Concurrency guard: skip spawn if an active run for (issueId, role) already exists.

Convoy reviewers (security/correctness/performance/requirements) move from being separate Cloister specialists (with prompt templates at src/lib/cloister/prompts/review/*.prompt-template.md) to Claude Code subagent definitions at .claude/agents/code-review-<flavor>.md, invoked from inside roles/review.md via the Agent tool. Synthesis IS the review role itself — no separate synthesis sub-agent. Each sub-reviewer's model resolves through resolveModel('review', '<flavor>').

Pi harness scope: PAN-1048 ships claude-code-only roles. getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand keeps its existing `pi --mode rpc --model <model>` short-circuit unchanged — Pi has no agent-definition system. A follow-up issue (e.g. PAN-1049) tracks Pi role-body integration. Settings still allows Pi as a per-role harness override; Pi just doesn't read the role's .md body.

Workhorse and per-role/per-sub-role model pickers list models from ALL enabled providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Z.AI/glm, Kimi, OpenRouter), grouped by provider with provider header rows. Maximum flexibility: workhorses.cheap = kimi-k2.6-flash and workhorses.expensive = glm-4.7-pro are both valid configurations.

Unify `runtime` → `harness` in this PR. The Channels eligibility check (src/lib/agents.ts ~line 775) moves from `state.runtime !== 'claude' && state.runtime !== 'claude-code'` to `state.harness === 'claude-code' && state.role === 'work'` — Channels was always work-agent-only, so the role check makes the intent explicit. M1 carries this; if it gets dropped on the floor, Channels silently breaks for every work agent.

Synthesis IS the review role — there is no separate synthesis sub-agent. The review role reads the diff, fans out 4 parallel convoy sub-runs via the Agent tool, collects their findings, decides approve/request-changes, posts the review comment, and transitions the issue state. Human-merge invariant: review NEVER merges; only approves and transitions.

Test discipline: every refactor bead must keep the existing tests green. If a test references a deleted enum or field, the bead that deletes the enum/field is responsible for updating or replacing the test. No 'test cleanup' bead at the end — tests are part of each bead's acceptance criteria.

Concurrent work runs (PAN-970 swarm parallelism) is out of scope for PAN-1048. The reactive scheduler holds a per-issue concurrency guard (no second run spawned while one is active for the same role on the same issue). The role primitive does not block PAN-970 — it makes it easier to build.

PRD location: this planning session left the PRD at .pan/prd.md (workspace-local). The final bead (z2-move-prd-canonical) moves it to docs/prds/planned/PAN-1048-role-primitive.md so the link in the GitHub issue body works after merge. Do not move it earlier — the PRD is mutated repeatedly during planning.

Deferred X1 legacy .claude/agents/pan-*-agent.md deletion because bd surfaced it before all spawn callsites had migrated to role-based launch. The files are still required by planning and specialist dispatch, so deleting them now would brick active and future agents. Treat X1 as out-of-scope until a follow-up after role spawn migration is verified on main.

Role model foundation uses type-only Role import in config-yaml.ts to avoid a runtime circular dependency with agents.ts. mergeConfigs remains the config-load validation boundary, while derefWorkhorse/resolveModel are exported for role spawn and settings code.

Fixed the CLI build artifact layout instead of working around pan inspect: inspect-agent.ts resolves prompts relative to dist/cli, so build:cli must copy cloister prompts to dist/cli/prompts as well as dist/prompts.

C2 hard-deleted legacy specialist wake/session/queue machinery while preserving on-demand ephemeral specialists required for inspect/reviewer flows. Review prompt sources now live exclusively as Claude Code agent definitions under .claude/agents/code-review-*.md; code-review-synthesis.md must be force-added because .claude/agents/ is ignored by default.

hazards.summary: WorkTypeId has 167 callsites; SpecialistType has 138. The work agent must update every callsite as it migrates through D1 + E2; missed callsites surface as TypeScript errors but the volume is high.
hazards.mitigation: After each enum-deletion bead, run `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` and fix every error before moving on. Don't suppress errors with `as any`. Each enum-deletion bead's acceptance criteria includes 'typecheck passes'.

hazards.summary: The convoy reviewer migration (V1 → R3 → S3) changes the spawn mechanism for 4 parallel reviewers. If R3's roles/review.md file does not correctly invoke .claude/agents/code-review-<flavor>.md sub-agents, the review pipeline silently degrades to no-convoy single-reviewer.
hazards.mitigation: S3 includes an end-to-end smoke test (subItem ac5) that validates the full convoy fan-out runs and produces a synthesized decision. R3 has requiresInspection=true so the inspect specialist verifies the file structure before downstream beads land.

hazards.summary: M1 (state.json hard cut) is the data-model break point. If any code path missed in earlier beads still reads state.type/state.agentPhase/state.phase/state.workType/state.complexity/state.handoffCount/state.runtime, the dashboard or spawn paths will break at runtime even though typecheck passes (string-property access on `any`-typed values).
hazards.mitigation: M1 acceptance criterion ac5 explicitly requires `grep returns zero hits in src/` for each deleted field. The work agent must run that grep and resolve every hit before signaling done. This is enforced by the Jidoka inspect for M1 (requiresInspection=true).

hazards.summary: Channels eligibility check at src/lib/agents.ts ~line 775 is easy to miss when deleting `runtime` from AgentState. If Channels silently disables for all work agents, prompt delivery falls back to tmux paste-buffer (not broken, but defeats the experimental Channels feature).
hazards.mitigation: M1 acceptance criterion ac3 explicitly requires moving the Channels gate from state.runtime to `state.harness === 'claude-code' && state.role === 'work'`. The bead description calls this out. Tests in tests/channels-eligibility (or similar) must continue to pass.

hazards.summary: The reactive Cloister scheduler depends on issue-lifecycle events that already exist plus agent-completion events that may not be emitted today. If C1 wires up subscribers for events that nothing fires, transitions break — work completes but review never spawns.
hazards.mitigation: Before C1 implementation, the work agent should grep for each event name (agent.completed, work.completed, review.approved, test.passed) and confirm an emitter exists. If a needed event is missing, C1's scope expands to add the emitter — this is a known acceptable scope creep, called out in C1's narrative. C1 has requiresInspection=true.

hazards.summary: The startup migration in M1 kills in-flight agents whose state.json lacks `role`. If a user has long-running agents at the moment they upgrade, those agents are terminated with no warning. Hard-cut decision (D1) accepted this trade-off.
hazards.mitigation: M1 logs the count of dropped agents at startup. Document the upgrade behavior in the release notes / CLAUDE.md update (z1-update-claude-md-and-docs). Recommend users pause active issues before upgrading.

hazards.summary: Settings UI rewrite (U1-U5) and backend deletion (D1, E1-E5, K1) are loosely coupled — UI assumes config has workhorses/roles fields, backend assumes UI sends new field shapes. If U5 (API settings) lands without the frontend updates, GET /api/settings returns the new shape but the old AgentCardsPanel chokes.
hazards.mitigation: U3 (settings page rewire) blocks U4 (delete old components). UI changes land in a single bead-cluster (U1 → U2,U5 → U3 → U4) with the page rewire as a single atomic point of switch. Manual UAT after U3 lands and again after U4 deletes the old code.

hazards.summary: Untracked files in the workspace (.claude/skills/pan-tts/scripts/tts_daemon.py, .claude/skills/pipeline-status/, three deleted feedback files) are unrelated to PAN-1048. Risk: a bead's `git add` accidentally sweeps them in.
hazards.mitigation: Work agent should never use `git add -A` or `git add .`. Always add specific files by name. The .pan/feedback/* deletions were already on disk at planning start and should be either committed in a separate cleanup or restored — the work agent should not touch them.

hazards.summary: The Cloister machinery deletion in C2 deletes ~30+ functions across specialists.ts, router.ts, deacon.ts, and the prompts/review/ directory. A missed reference to one of those deleted functions (e.g., from a test fixture or an unused-but-imported module) causes typecheck to fail.
hazards.mitigation: C2 acceptance criterion ac8 requires typecheck + lint + tests pass. The bead description lists every function/file to delete. Work agent runs the gates after each subset of deletions, not at the end.

hazards.summary: Contracts migration (E2, K1) crosses the wire-format boundary between server and frontend. If contracts are rebuilt but the frontend isn't recompiled (Vite dev cache, stale dist), runtime errors appear that look like backend bugs.
hazards.mitigation: K1 acceptance criterion ac3 requires `cd packages/contracts && npm run build` followed by a frontend rebuild. Acceptance criterion ac4 requires an end-to-end smoke (open dashboard, observe a running role) before signaling done.

hazards.summary: The bead dependency materialization allowed X1 to become ready too early, despite runtime dependencies on legacy agent definition files.
hazards.mitigation: Do not touch .claude/agents/pan-*-agent.md in PAN-1048 unless the vBRIEF and bd graph are corrected after S2-S5/C1 land. Keep legacy files in place until a separate cleanup issue verifies role-based launch is stable on main.

hazards.summary: The bd dependency graph for PAN-1048 was materialized in reverse, causing terminal cleanup beads to appear before foundation beads.
hazards.mitigation: Corrected the active bd graph enough for f1-role-config-schema to be the sole ready bead before implementing it; continue checking bd ready against the vBRIEF order and fix dependency edges before executing any bead that appears before its prerequisites.

hazards.summary: `pan inspect` failed when dist/cli/prompts/inspect-agent.md was missing even though dist/prompts/inspect-agent.md existed.
hazards.mitigation: scripts/build-cli.mjs now copies prompt templates into both locations; run npm run build:cli before requesting inspection if dist is stale.

resumePoint.description: All executable PAN-1048 beads are closed; workspace-bnrg/X1 remains explicitly deferred. pan done initially blocked on deferred X1 acceptance criteria, so the completion gate was fixed to ignore deferred/cancelled vBRIEF items when extracting ACs. Typecheck, lint, full tests, and graphify update pass; commit/push and pan done are next.

sessionHistory.content: Planning session for PAN-1048 (Unify agent type system: 5 enums → Role primitive). Pre-planning, the user updated the PRD and GitHub issue with the new Workhorse Models concept (3 user-managed slots — expensive/mid/cheap — referenced from per-role and per-sub-role pickers via `workhorse:<slot>` indirection). The user also clarified that plan + review should both default to workhorse:expensive (planning errors cascade; review gates merge). Architectural decisions surfaced via AskUserQuestion: (1) drop work phases entirely; (2) reactive Cloister hooks into existing internal event emitters rather than building a new tracker poller; (3) hard cut on upgrade — kill in-flight agents, drop legacy fields, no readRole() shim; (4) workhorse pickers list all enabled providers grouped; (5) Pi parity deferred to follow-up; (6) convoy prompt templates migrate to .claude/agents/code-review-<flavor>.md invoked via the Agent tool; (7) runtime → harness unification in this PR. Codebase mapping confirmed: 138 SpecialistType callsites, 167 WorkTypeId callsites, no `roles/` directory exists yet, IssueLifecycle service already emits issue.* domain events, convoy reviewers today are separate Cloister specialists (not subagents). Decomposed into 32 beads with 57 dependency edges across 7 phases (Foundation, Convoy migration, Role files, Per-role spawn wiring, Reactive Cloister + machinery deletion, Settings UI, Backend dead-code deletion + enum deletions + contracts + state.json hard cut + cleanup).
sessionHistory.note: Planning complete. 32 beads written to .pan/spec.vbrief.json. Continue.json populated with 13 decisions and 10 hazards.
Moved .pan/prd.md to docs/prds/planned/PAN-1048-role-primitive.md, marked z2-move-prd-canonical acceptance criteria complete, and left .pan/prd.md absent from the working tree.

Deferred workspace-bnrg/X1 after user correction: legacy pan agent definition files are still required by current planning and specialist spawn paths. Reverted accidental code-comment edits and preserved only the vBRIEF deferral metadata.

Implemented Role type plus workhorse/role model config schema, derefWorkhorse, resolveModel, config-load validation through mergeConfigs, and vitest coverage for role resolution, sub-role override, missing-slot and chained-ref errors. Typecheck and targeted role config tests pass.
Fixed build:cli to copy cloister prompt templates to dist/cli/prompts, resolving pan inspect prompt-template lookup failure without manually copying artifacts.

Implemented K1 contracts cleanup: replaced AgentPhase with Role in contracts, removed SpecialistSnapshot export, moved agent enrichment payloads to role, updated reducer/read-model/frontend store consumers, rebuilt contracts, verified root typecheck, frontend build, and role event projection smoke.

Implemented U5 Settings API support for top-level workhorses and roles. GET seeds defaults and omits legacy overrides; PUT writes workhorses/roles via yaml.parseDocument to preserve comments and delete models.overrides; validation rejects unknown roles/sub-roles plus invalid, chained, and unresolved model refs. Added settings-api coverage and verified targeted test, typecheck, and lint pass.

Implemented F2 AgentState role field as an additive optional Role on AgentState. Existing JSON parse/save path already preserves unknown typed fields, and a new vitest proves role persists through state.json. Verified targeted test, typecheck, and lint pass.

Implemented F3 default workhorse/role seeding in config-yaml: DEFAULT_WORKHORSES and DEFAULT_ROLES are in-memory defaults, mergeConfigs clones them before overlaying user config, and partial role/workhorse configs preserve user values while seeding missing entries. Settings API now consumes the shared defaults. Targeted config/settings tests, typecheck, and lint pass.

Implemented V1 convoy reviewer migration by copying the four review prompt templates into .claude/agents/code-review-{security,correctness,performance,requirements}.md and adding a smoke test that validates frontmatter shape plus body parity with the source templates. Source prompt-template files were not deleted. Targeted test, typecheck, and lint pass.

Implemented R1 plan role file at roles/plan.md with Claude Code frontmatter, migrated planning workflow, explicit pan plan-finalize and stop instructions, and retained the legacy .claude/agents/pan-planning-agent.md. Added role definition smoke coverage. Targeted test, typecheck, and lint pass.

Implemented R2 work role with one undifferentiated work mode, per-bead workflow, universal inspect and requiresInspection-only inspect-deep Jidoka gates, explicit resolveModel(work, subRole) contract, and inspect/inspect-deep Claude Code subagent definitions. Legacy pan-work-agent and pan-inspect-agent remain. Targeted role test, typecheck, and lint pass.

Implemented S2 work role end-to-end: SpawnOptions accepts role: work; spawnAgent writes role instead of legacy phase/workType/complexity/handoff fields; determineModel now routes through resolveModel(work); work launches use --agent roles/work.md; dashboard POST /api/agents rejects legacy workType/phase/agentType fields and Kanban sends role: work. Updated CLI/dashboard spawn callers, handoff spawn, and launcher/model tests. Verified targeted tests, typecheck, lint, and graphify update.

Implemented S1 plan role end-to-end: planning state writes role: plan instead of type/agentPhase/phase; model resolution uses resolveModel(plan) via roles/workhorses config; planning Claude Code launch maps to --agent roles/plan.md; launcher env uses PANOPTICON_SESSION_TYPE=plan; AgentOutputPanel and read-model consume state.role without legacy planning field fallbacks; PlanDialog displays the default from roles.plan.model. Verified targeted planning/role tests, typecheck, lint, and graphify update.

Implemented R3 review role definition at roles/review.md. The review role is synthesis (no separate synthesis sub-agent), dispatches code-review-security/correctness/performance/requirements in parallel via Agent tool calls, documents synthesis decision flow, review state transition responsibilities, resolveModel(review, subRole) routing, and the invariant that review NEVER merges. Legacy .claude/agents/pan-review-agent.md remains. Verified role definition test, typecheck, lint, and graphify update.

Implemented U1 WorkhorsePanel settings UI with three accessible workhorse dropdowns (Expensive/Mid/Cheap), provider-grouped options from /api/settings/available-models, settings read/PUT round-trip behavior, and inline slot descriptions. Integrated WorkhorsePanel at the top of Settings model routing and added focused component tests. Verified targeted test, typecheck, lint, and graphify update.

Implemented R5 ship role definition at roles/ship.md. The role prepares an approved PR for human merge by fetching/rebasing onto main, resolving conflicts, running typecheck/lint/test, pushing the feature branch, and transitioning the issue/PR to ready-to-merge. It explicitly bans gh pr merge, merge API POSTs, git merge into main, direct/force pushes to main, and hook bypasses; legacy pan-merge-agent remains. Verified role definition test, typecheck, lint, and graphify update.

Implemented R4 test role definition at roles/test.md. The role runs configured project verification and folds browser UAT into the same role when continue.json, vBRIEF acceptance criteria, PR notes, or issue text require end-to-end proof. It declares Playwright MCP availability, requires an isolated browser instance per session, emits TESTS PASSED/TESTS FAILED sentinels, and keeps legacy pan-test-agent and pan-uat-agent definitions. Verified role definition test, typecheck, lint, and graphify update.

Implemented S3 review role wiring. Added generic spawnRun(issueId, role, opts) for role-based runs, roleAgentDefinitionPath/getRoleRuntimeBaseCommand helpers that launch roles/review.md, and launcher type support for test/ship role runs. Migrated review handoff and dashboard approval review pipeline from legacy review-agent wake to spawnRun(issueId, review). Verified no convoy specialist wake callsites, added role runtime command coverage, and ran targeted tests, typecheck, lint, and graphify update.

Implemented U2 RolesPanel with five role cards in order, work/review sub-role expansion, live-resolved workhorse option labels and tooltips, provider-grouped specific model options, and settings GET/PUT persistence for roles.<id>.model plus roles.<id>.sub.<sub>.model. Targeted RolesPanel tests, typecheck, and lint pass.

Implemented S5 ship role wiring. spawnMergeAgentForBranches/spawnRebaseAgentForBranch and sync-main conflict delegation now start spawnRun(issueId, 'ship') with role prompts that rebase, verify, push, and POST readyForMerge=true without merging. Removed legacy merge-agent specialist wake paths from merge-agent.ts and updated merge-agent tests to assert ship role launch plus postMergeLifecycle stash cleanup. Targeted merge-agent test, typecheck, lint, and graphify update pass.

Implemented S4 test role wiring. test-agent-queue now starts spawnRun(issueId, 'test') with a prompt that runs project gates, performs Playwright UAT when required, and posts testStatus/readyForMerge updates. review-status, specialists auto-complete, and Deacon retry/orphan recovery now dispatch the test role instead of test-agent specialists; no uat-agent spawn remains. AgentOutputPanel explicitly renders test role runs from state.role === 'test'. Targeted dispatch/panel tests, typecheck, lint, and graphify update pass.

Implemented C1 reactive Cloister scheduler: stateToRole maps issue states to Role, onIssueStateChange launches spawnRun with a concurrency guard, Cloister subscribes to domain events, contracts define agent.completed/work.completed/review.approved/test.passed, review status transitions emit review.approved/test.passed, legacy status routes no longer directly dispatch test after review pass, and tests cover work completion → review, review approval → test, and test pass → ship transitions. Targeted tests, typecheck, lint, and graphify update completed.

Implemented workspace-ifod/C2: removed legacy specialist wake/queue/session machinery, deleted resolveSpecialistBaseCommand and source review prompt templates, added code-review-synthesis agent definition, converted specialist wake/reset/init surfaces to unsupported responses, routed deacon orphan test recovery through spawnRun(test), updated stale sync-main/settings/read-model/test-delivery/post-merge tests for role-based behavior, and cleaned lingering deleted-specialist mock/comment references in tests. Verified npx tsc --noEmit, npx eslint . --max-warnings=0, full npm test (383 passed/5 skipped test files; 4605 passed/62 skipped tests), and graphify update pass.

Implemented workspace-jdu7/Z1 docs: added the role taxonomy to CLAUDE.md, rewrote harness and agent architecture docs around plan/work/review/test/ship roles and sub-roles, updated kanban/review architecture language away from specialist machinery, and refreshed the planning prompt taxonomy so planners encode downstream requirements for roles rather than named specialists. Verified JSON artifacts, npx tsc --noEmit, npx eslint . --max-warnings=0, full npm test (383 passed/5 skipped test files; 4605 passed/62 skipped tests), and graphify update.

Implemented workspace-00df/D1: deleted WorkType registry/router modules and their direct tests, migrated backend/dashboard/settings/review/specialist/router-config callsites from WorkTypeId/WORK_TYPES/WorkTypeRouter/getModelId/hasOverride to role-based config-yaml resolveModel(role, subRole?) or opaque legacy model-route strings where compatibility surfaces remain, retained smart-model-selector.ts as a decoupled research utility because it has a non-router caller, rewrote config precedence coverage for role/workhorse resolution, and verified npx tsc --noEmit, npx eslint . --max-warnings=0, full npm test (381 passed/5 skipped test files; 4530 passed/62 skipped tests), source/packages/tests grep for legacy symbols, and graphify update.

Implemented workspace-9uxn/U3: SettingsPage now renders WorkhorsePanel followed by RolesPanel in the model-routing section, and a SettingsPage test asserts that order while guarding that the old AgentCardsPanel route is not mounted from SettingsPage. Verified npx tsc --noEmit, npx eslint . --max-warnings=0, full npm test (381 passed/5 skipped test files; 4531 passed/62 skipped tests), and graphify update.

Implemented workspace-3mhb/U4: deleted the legacy Settings AgentCards override UI components (AgentCardsPanel, AgentCard, ModelOverrideModal, AgentCards index) and WorkTypeOverrides, removed SettingsPage legacy route-card override UI code, preserved reusable provider/model catalog data in modelCatalog.ts for remaining Settings selectors, updated tests to use the new catalog module, verified deleted-component references are gone except guard assertions, and ran npx tsc --noEmit, npx eslint . --max-warnings=0, full npm test (381 passed/5 skipped test files; 4531 passed/62 skipped tests), and graphify update.

Implemented workspace-afb9/E1: deleted the PanopticonAgentType export and panopticonAgentName helper from agents.ts, made getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand accept direct agent definition strings instead of translating an agent enum, updated work/planning recovery, planning spawn, review-agent, and specialist command callsites to pass roles/work.md, roles/plan.md, or explicit pan-* agent names, and updated command-builder tests. Verified no PanopticonAgentType/panopticonAgentName references remain, npx tsc --noEmit, npx eslint . --max-warnings=0, targeted runtime-command tests (31 passed), full npm test (381 passed/5 skipped test files; 4531 passed/62 skipped tests), and graphify update.

Implemented workspace-gil3/E2: deleted SpecialistType, SpecialistMetadata, and SpecialistStatus from specialists.ts by replacing remaining legacy runtime surfaces with SpecialistAgentName/LegacySpecialistDefinition/LegacySpecialistRuntimeStatus; removed contract SpecialistType/SpecialistState schemas and SpecialistSnapshot references; changed specialist domain events to use Role names; mapped legacy specialist route/report emissions to review/test/ship; updated read-model, websocket/activity consumers, CLI/frontend/tests, and removed all remaining deleted specialist type name references. Verified contracts build via npm test pretest, npm run typecheck, npm run lint, full npm test (381 passed/5 skipped test files; 4531 passed/62 skipped tests), deleted-symbol grep, and graphify update.

Implemented workspace-3onu/E4: confirmed WorkTypeId/WORK_TYPES references are gone, deleted orphaned legacy Settings model-route files (modelDefaults.ts and WorkTypeTable.tsx), removed ModelRouteId/WORK_TYPE_CATEGORIES/getOptimalModelDefaults and convoy override-key migration surfaces, cleaned remaining legacy route-string grep hits from src/packages, and verified npm run typecheck, npm run lint, full npm test (381 passed/5 skipped test files; 4521 passed/62 skipped tests), required greps, and graphify update.

Implemented workspace-d07q/E5: deleted the ActivitySource union from activity-logger, constrained normal activity sources to Role plus cloister/dashboard, migrated plan/work/review/test/ship activity emitters and detailed activity mapping to role source names, removed deploy-script/legacy specialist source rendering from ActivityPanel, and updated tests. Verified npm run typecheck, npm run lint, full npm test (381 passed/5 skipped test files; 4518 passed/62 skipped tests), and graphify update.

Implemented workspace-tgba/E3: deleted LauncherAgentType, refactored LauncherConfig to require role: Role plus optional spawnMode for conversation/remote/resume, removed specialist-dispatch and specialist-init branches from launcher-generator, migrated launcher call sites/tests to role/spawnMode, and verified npm run typecheck, npm run lint, full npm test (381 passed/5 skipped test files; 4518 passed/62 skipped tests), and graphify update.

Implemented workspace-f3us/M1: removed legacy AgentState fields (type/agentPhase/phase/workType/complexity/handoffCount/runtime), made role required, sanitized parse/save round-trips to the new schema, added startup dropping of no-role legacy agent state with best-effort tmux kill and count logging, moved Channels eligibility to state.role === work plus harness === claude-code with updated reason strings, migrated CLI/dashboard/deacon/health/cost/runtime callsites and tests away from legacy state fields, and verified npm run typecheck, npm run lint, full npm test (381 passed/5 skipped test files; 4521 passed/62 skipped tests), forbidden state-field grep, and graphify update.

Fixed pan done completion gating after PAN-1048 executable beads closed: vBRIEF acceptance criteria extraction now skips deferred/cancelled plan items so intentionally deferred X1 cleanup ACs do not block issue completion. Added regression coverage for deferred items and verified npm run typecheck, npm run lint, full npm test (381 passed/5 skipped test files; 4523 passed/62 skipped tests), and graphify update.
Unified agent state and routing around required Role primitive; deferred cleanup no longer blocks completion; quality gates passed.

Fixed pan done stale merged-status guard: cached review status can say mergeStatus=merged even when the feature branch is not an ancestor of the target branch. pan done now verifies git ancestry for the review artifact merge set before skipping the review pipeline, and falls through to reset review/test/merge status when the cached status is stale. Added command-level regression coverage and verified npm run typecheck, npm run lint, targeted doneCommand tests, full npm test (383 passed/5 skipped test files; 4550 passed/62 skipped tests), and graphify update.
